description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Year of the current NFL season, if we are in the mid-season. If we are in the off-season, then year of the next upcoming season. This value changes immediately after the Super Bowl. NFL seasons run across two calendar years; the league year is the one in which it starts, not ends (that is, a season starting in 2023 and ending in 2024 will have the league year of 2023.)

(Required) Abbreviation of the team. Example: WAS.

(Required) Year of the season (with optional season type).
Examples: 2020REG, 2020PRE, 2020POST, 2021, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).
(Required) The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

response.name: Success

Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given GameID.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

item.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds - by Week [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds - by Week [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns the full-game and non-full-game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games in a given week and season. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season, with optional season type.
Examples: 2018, 2018POST, etc.

(Required) The week of the scores (games).
Examples: 1, 2, etc.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

item.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns the game odds (for games in a given week and season. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The ScoreID of an NFL score (game). ScoreIDs can be found in the Scores API. Valid entries are 16654 or 16667

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games in a given week and season. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. As this is in-game, it will only return results while the game is in progress. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

item.name: Betting Player Props - by Game [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Betting Player Props - by Game [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The unique ScoreID of the game in question.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The unique ScoreID of the game in question.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

item.name: Betting Markets - by Market Type [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Betting Markets - by Market Type [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).
(Required) The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

response.name: Success

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Returns the markets and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines.

A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Provide a market ID that supports resulting (i.e. has a ResultType) and this endpoint will return a result: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

(Required) The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Returns available Futures markets for the given season. Does not include line movement. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season (with optional season type).
Examples: 2020REG, 2020PRE, 2020POST, 2021, etc.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

response.name: Success

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

description.content:
Returns the game odds ( for games in a given week and season. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included.

This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost.

Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line.

A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The ScoreID of an NFL score (game). ScoreIDs can be found in the Scores API. Valid entries are 16654 or 16667

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100
(Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The ScoreID of an NFL score (game). ScoreIDs can be found in the Scores API. Valid entries are 16654 or 16667

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

item.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Provides in-play odds line movement data for a given game. This means odds for games which are in-progress. Serves full line movement and is intended for showing the trend over a game rather than the most up-to-the second lines.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The ScoreID of an NFL score (game). ScoreIDs can be found in the Scores API. Valid entries are 16654 or 16667

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100
(Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The ScoreID of an NFL score (game). ScoreIDs can be found in the Scores API. Valid entries are 16654 or 16667

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
This method returns all player game stats, but only returns player stats that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. By definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1 or 2.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1 or 2.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of the team. Example: WAS.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Player Game Stats Delta - by Week

request.name: Player Game Stats Delta - by Week

description.content: This method returns all player scores for a given season and week, but only returns player stats that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. Ideal for live applications.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1 or 2.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1 or 2.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1 or 2.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

This method returns all box scores for a given season and week, but only returns player stats that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. You can also filter by type of player stats to include, such as traditional fantasy players, IDP players or all players. by definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) The subcategory of players to include in the returned PlayerGame records. Possible values include:

all Returns all players
fantasy Returns traditional fantasy players (QB, RB, WR, TE, K, DST)
idp Returns traditional fantasy players and IDP players.

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1, 2, etc.

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) The subcategory of players to include in the returned PlayerGame records. Possible values include:

all Returns all players
fantasy Returns traditional fantasy players (QB, RB, WR, TE, K, DST)
idp Returns traditional fantasy players and IDP players.

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1, 2, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered live during the game, called for a given team's game in a given week and season.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of a team playing in this game. Example: WAS.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of a team playing in this game. Example: WAS.

item.name: Fantasy Player Ownership Percentages (Season-Long) - by Week

request.name: Fantasy Player Ownership Percentages (Season-Long) - by Week

description.content: Projected fantasy ownership of all players for a given season.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

item.name: Player Season Red Zone Stats (Inside Five)

request.name: Player Season Red Zone Stats (Inside Five)

description.content: Delivers all offensive stats within the 5-yard line of the defensive team for all players in a given season (i.e. the season total, not each individual game record.)

Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.

Specify a season, a player, and number of games (either an integer or all) to see all of their box score logs. Refreshed after their most recent game is complete.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Unique SportsDataIO Player ID. Example:17920.
(Required) How many games to return. Example all, 10, 25

(Required) How many games to return. Example all, 10, 25

(Required) The ScoreID of the game. Possible values include: 16247, 16245, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a specified game week for each game that took place, down to the team and player stat level, delivered after the game is complete.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of the team. Example: WAS.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a given team's game in a specified season and week, down to the team and player stat level, delivered after the game is complete.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of a team playing in this game. Example: WAS.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of a team playing in this game. Example: WAS.

Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of the home team. Example: WAS.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Gets simulated live play-by-play of NFL games, covering the Conference Championship games on January 21, 2018.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The number of plays to progress in this NFL live game simulation. Example entries are 0, 1, 2, 3, 150, 200, etc.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The number of plays to progress in this NFL live game simulation. Example entries are 0, 1, 2, 3, 150, 200, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

This method returns all play-by-plays for a given season and week, but only returnsplays that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. by definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1 or 2.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1 or 2.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1 or 2.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Each invididual play, its type and outcome, complete with player and team stats down to the play level, delivered final (after the game ends), called by team.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The GameID of a NFL game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14620 or 16905

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The GameID of a NFL game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14620 or 16905

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Each invididual play, its type and outcome, complete with player and team stats down to the play level, delivered final (after the game ends), called by team.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of the home team. Example: WAS.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of the home team. Example: WAS.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.
(Required) Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1
(Required) Abbreviation of the home team. Example: WAS.
